verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Benchmark run; Overhead within noise; Consumes T2.7 harness; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gate executed red with the released benchmark artifact absent (2/3 failing; the live-bundle capability test passed because the ARCHBP-021 bundle already existed — recorded honestly), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.benchmark_evidence: The T2.7 harness (scripts/envelope-latency-bench.mjs, unchanged) ran against the RELEASED bundle launcher via YZX_ENVELOPE_BIN=released-engine-shim.sh — the ARCHBP-021 envelope --store mode with the host store hidden, on a 36-path/234MB closure including python3. Nine interleaved self-timed runs per arm.
gate_result.overhead_evidence: bare 1.0293s vs released-envelope 1.0364s median -> runtime overhead 0.69%, envelope setup ~33ms one-time (reported separately, excluded from the runtime claim). ARCHBP-130 measured the dev-host envelope at -0.04%; both land within measurement noise — the release vehicle is a native-process namespace, not a hypervisor (I04): no hypervisor tax.
gate_result.harness_evidence: planning-spine-v0/docs/envelope_released_benchmark.json carries consumes=scripts/envelope-latency-bench.mjs and the harness's own schema (lifeos-planning-spine.envelope-latency-bench.v0); the harness was not forked or modified.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Real released-bundle envelope launches (host store hidden, python3 resolved from the bundle closure alone) with the workload self-timing inside the released environment; the gate re-executes a live released-bundle run on every suite pass.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-120 is complete: the released envelope was benchmarked against bare native through the unchanged T2.7 harness — 0.69% runtime overhead with ~33ms one-time setup, within measurement noise and consistent with the dev-host envelope's -0.04% — confirming no hypervisor tax for the release vehicle.
